name: Create SKILL.md
description:
Create the skill/claude-code/SKILL.md file at the project root. This is the Claude Code skill that ships with devrig. It must include:

1. YAML frontmatter with:
   - name: devrig
   - description: comprehensive trigger description covering service health, errors, performance, traces, logs, metrics, debugging, slow requests
   - allowed-tools: Bash(devrig *)

2. Markdown body (under 300 lines) with:
   - Available commands section covering all devrig query subcommands (traces, trace, logs, metrics, status, related)
   - Service management commands (devrig ps, devrig restart, devrig env, devrig k)
   - Output format notes (NDJSON default, --format json-pretty for readable output)
   - Three workflow guides:
     a. Debugging performance issues (slow traces → span detail → related → logs)
     b. Investigating errors (error traces → error logs → cross-reference with related)
     c. Checking system health (status → metrics → warn/error logs)

The file must reference only real devrig CLI subcommands. Every command example must use the actual flag names from src/cli.rs (e.g., --service, --status, --min-duration, --level, --search, --trace-id, --format, --limit, --last).
validation: test -f skill/claude-code/SKILL.md && head -5 skill/claude-code/SKILL.md | grep -q 'name: devrig'

name: Extend config model with AddonConfig
description:
Add addon configuration types to src/config/model.rs:

1. Create AddonConfig enum with #[serde(tag = "type")]:
   - Helm variant: chart (String), repo (String), namespace (String), version (Option<String>), values (BTreeMap<String, toml::Value>), port_forward (BTreeMap<u16, String>)
   - Manifest variant: path (String), namespace (Option<String>), port_forward (BTreeMap<u16, String>)
   - Kustomize variant: path (String), namespace (Option<String>), port_forward (BTreeMap<u16, String>)

2. Add `addons: BTreeMap<String, AddonConfig>` to ClusterConfig with #[serde(default)] for backwards compatibility.

3. AddonConfig must derive Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq.

4. Add Serialize derive to toml::Value import if needed (toml::Value already implements Serialize).

5. Add unit tests:
   - parse_addon_helm_config: Parse a full [cluster.addons.traefik] with type="helm", chart, repo, namespace, values, port_forward
   - parse_addon_manifest_config: Parse [cluster.addons.my-tool] with type="manifest"
   - parse_addon_kustomize_config: Parse [cluster.addons.overlay] with type="kustomize"
   - parse_cluster_without_addons: Existing cluster config still parses (backwards compat)
   - parse_addon_port_forward_map: Verify port_forward = { 9000 = "svc/traefik:9000" } parses correctly
   - parse_addon_with_values: Verify values with mixed types (string, bool, int) parse correctly

name: Add addon validation rules
description:
Extend src/config/validate.rs with addon-specific validation:

1. Add new ConfigDiagnostic variants:
   - EmptyAddonChart: addon has type=helm but empty chart string
   - EmptyAddonRepo: addon has type=helm but empty repo string
   - EmptyAddonNamespace: addon has type=helm but empty namespace string
   - EmptyAddonPath: addon has type=manifest or kustomize but empty path string
   - AddonPortConflict: addon port_forward local port conflicts with service/infra/dashboard port
   - DuplicateAddonPort: two addons use the same local port_forward port
   - AddonNameConflict: addon name conflicts with a cluster.deploy name

2. In the validate() function, iterate over cluster.addons (if cluster is Some) and:
   - Check non-empty required fields per variant
   - Collect all addon port_forward local ports and check against the existing port conflict detection
   - Check addon names don't duplicate deploy names

3. Add unit tests:
   - validate_addon_empty_chart: Helm addon with empty chart produces error
   - validate_addon_port_conflict_with_service: Addon port_forward port same as service port
   - validate_addon_name_conflict_with_deploy: Addon and deploy share a name
   - validate_valid_addon_config: Valid addon config passes validation

name: Extend state model with AddonState
description:
Extend src/orchestrator/state.rs:

1. Add AddonState struct:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   pub struct AddonState {
       pub addon_type: String,
       pub namespace: String,
       pub installed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
   }
   ```

2. Add to ClusterState:
   ```rust
   #[serde(default)]
   pub installed_addons: BTreeMap<String, AddonState>,
   ```

This uses #[serde(default)] for backwards compatibility with existing state.json files from v0.5.

name: Implement skill install command
description:
Implement the devrig skill install CLI command:

1. Create src/commands/skill.rs with:
   - const SKILL_MD: &str = include_str!("../../skill/claude-code/SKILL.md");
   - pub async fn run_install(global: bool, config_file: Option<&Path>) -> Result<()>
   - If global: target = home_dir/.claude/skills/devrig/
   - If not global: target = config_dir/.claude/skills/devrig/ (where config_dir is the directory containing devrig.toml, resolved from config_file or CWD walk)
   - Create target directory with tokio::fs::create_dir_all
   - Write SKILL.md to target/SKILL.md
   - Print success message with target path and example prompt
   - Idempotent: overwrite existing files on re-install

2. Update src/commands/mod.rs: add `pub mod skill;`

3. Update src/cli.rs:
   - Add Skill variant to Commands enum with subcommand SkillCommands
   - SkillCommands::Install { #[arg(long)] global: bool }

4. Update src/main.rs:
   - Add dispatch for Commands::Skill { command } => match command { SkillCommands::Install { global } => commands::skill::run_install(global, cli.global.config_file.as_deref()).await }

Note: Use dirs::home_dir() or std::env::var("HOME") for the home directory. The dirs crate is not a dependency — use env::var("HOME") which is simpler and already works in the codebase.
validation: cargo build && cargo run -- skill install --help

name: Add helm to devrig doctor
description:
Extend src/commands/doctor.rs to check for helm:

1. Add ("helm", &["version", "--short"]) to the checks array in the run() function.

2. Helm 3 outputs like 'v3.14.0+g3fc9f72' and Helm 4 outputs like 'v4.0.0+g...'.

This follows the exact same pattern as the existing k3d, kubectl, docker checks.

name: Implement addon lifecycle module
description:
Create src/cluster/addon.rs with the addon install/uninstall/port-forward lifecycle:

1. Helper function: run_helm() — executes helm with KUBECONFIG env var, captures output, respects CancellationToken. Follow the run_cmd() pattern from src/cluster/deploy.rs.

2. toml_value_to_helm_set(value: &toml::Value) -> String — converts TOML values to helm --set format:
   - String → the string value
   - Boolean → "true" / "false"
   - Integer → numeric string
   - Float → numeric string
   - Array → "{val1,val2,val3}"

3. install_helm_addon(name, config, kubeconfig, cancel) -> Result<()>:
   - helm repo add {name} {repo} --force-update
   - helm repo update {name}
   - Build args: helm upgrade --install {name} {chart} --namespace {namespace} --create-namespace --wait --timeout 5m
   - Add --version if specified
   - Add --set key=value for each values entry
   - Run helm with args

4. install_manifest_addon(name, config, kubeconfig, config_dir, cancel) -> Result<()>:
   - Resolve path relative to config_dir
   - kubectl apply -f {path} with optional --namespace

5. install_kustomize_addon(name, config, kubeconfig, config_dir, cancel) -> Result<()>:
   - kubectl apply -k {path} with optional --namespace

6. install_addons(addons, kubeconfig, config_dir, cancel) -> Result<BTreeMap<String, AddonState>>:
   - Iterate addons in BTreeMap order (alphabetical)
   - Dispatch to appropriate installer based on variant
   - Return map of installed addon states

7. uninstall_addons(addons, kubeconfig, cancel) -> Result<()>:
   - For Helm addons: helm uninstall {name} --namespace {namespace}
   - For Manifest addons: kubectl delete -f {path}
   - For Kustomize addons: kubectl delete -k {path}
   - Errors are logged but don't stop other uninstalls

8. PortForwardManager struct:
   - tracker: TaskTracker
   - cancel: CancellationToken
   - start_port_forwards(addons, kubeconfig) -> spawns a task per port_forward entry
   - Each task runs a reconnection loop:
     ```
     loop {
         spawn kubectl port-forward --namespace {ns} {target} {local}:{remote} (kill_on_drop=true)
         select! {
             exit = child.wait() => { log + backoff + retry }
             _ = cancel.cancelled() => { kill child; break }
         }
     }
     ```
   - stop() -> cancel the token

9. Add unit tests for toml_value_to_helm_set:
   - string value
   - boolean true/false
   - integer
   - float
   - array of strings

10. Update src/cluster/mod.rs to add `pub mod addon;`

name: Integrate addons into orchestrator
description:
Modify src/orchestrator/mod.rs to wire addon lifecycle into the startup/shutdown phases:

1. In Phase 3.5 (after cluster creation, before service deploy):
   - After k3d cluster create succeeds
   - If config.cluster.addons is non-empty:
     a. Call addon::install_addons() with the addon configs, kubeconfig path, config dir, and cancel token
     b. Create PortForwardManager and call start_port_forwards()
     c. Store addon states in cluster_state.installed_addons
     d. Store PortForwardManager handle for shutdown

2. In stop():
   - If port_forward_manager exists, call stop()
   - Leave addons running (cluster is preserved on stop)

3. In delete():
   - If port_forward_manager exists, call stop()
   - If cluster has addons, call uninstall_addons() before k3d cluster delete

4. The PortForwardManager must be stored as an Option field on the Orchestrator struct or in a local variable that persists through the watch phase.

Key consideration: The existing orchestrator already tracks the K3dManager. The addon module functions should accept kubeconfig_path and cancel token as parameters, not require the full K3dManager. This keeps the interface clean.

name: Add addons to startup summary
description:
Modify src/ui/summary.rs to display installed addons in the startup summary:

1. Add an optional addons parameter to print_startup_summary() or create a new print_addon_info() function (following the pattern of print_dashboard_info()).

2. For each addon with port_forward entries, display:
   - Addon name
   - Port-forwarded URL(s): http://localhost:{local_port}
   - Status indicator (● ready)

3. For addons without port_forward, just show the name and status.

4. Example output:
   ```
     Addons
       traefik    http://localhost:9000 (dashboard)  ● ready
       cert-mgr                                      ● ready
   ```

5. Call this from the orchestrator after addon installation completes.

name: Implement config API endpoints
description:
Create src/dashboard/routes/config.rs with three endpoints:

1. GET /api/config:
   - Read the devrig.toml file from disk (path from DashboardState)
   - Compute SHA-256 hash of the content
   - Return JSON: { "content": "...", "path": "/path/to/devrig.toml", "hash": "abc123..." }

2. POST /api/config/validate:
   - Accept text/plain body with TOML content
   - Parse with toml::from_str::<DevrigConfig>()
   - If parse fails, return syntax error with line/column
   - If parse succeeds, run validate() with the parsed config
   - Return JSON: { "valid": true } or { "valid": false, "errors": [{"offset": N, "length": N, "severity": "error", "message": "..."}] }
   - Map miette diagnostic source spans to offset/length in the response

3. PUT /api/config:
   - Accept JSON body: { "content": "...", "hash": "..." }
   - Validate the content first (reject if invalid)
   - Read current file, compute hash, compare with request hash
   - If hash mismatch: return 409 Conflict with { "error": "conflict", "current_content": "...", "current_hash": "..." }
   - Create backup: devrig.toml.bak
   - Write atomically: write to .tmp, rename
   - Return 200 with { "hash": "new_hash" }

4. Update src/dashboard/routes/mod.rs:
   - Add `pub mod config;`
   - Add config routes to api_router()
   - Extend DashboardState with config_path: PathBuf

5. Update src/dashboard/server.rs:
   - Accept config_path parameter in start_dashboard_server()
   - Pass it to DashboardState

6. Update src/orchestrator/mod.rs Phase 4.5:
   - Pass config_path to start_dashboard_server()

Note: Use sha2::Sha256 (already a dependency) for hashing.

name: Install frontend dependencies
description:
Update dashboard/package.json to add CodeMirror and smol-toml dependencies, then install:

1. Add to dependencies in dashboard/package.json:
   - "solid-codemirror": "^2.3.1"
   - "@codemirror/state": "^6.5.4"
   - "@codemirror/view": "^6.38.8"
   - "@codemirror/language": "^6.0.0"
   - "@codemirror/legacy-modes": "^6.5.2"
   - "@codemirror/lint": "^6.9.4"
   - "@codemirror/theme-one-dark": "^6.0.0"
   - "codemirror": "^6.0.0"
   - "smol-toml": "^1.6.0"

2. Run `cd dashboard && npm install` to update node_modules and package-lock.json.

3. Verify the build still works: `cd dashboard && npm run build`.
validation: cd /home/steve/fj/devrig/dashboard && npm install && npm run build

name: Create config editor frontend component
description:
Create the CodeMirror-based config editor component and view:

1. Create dashboard/src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx:
   - Import createCodeMirror from solid-codemirror
   - Import StreamLanguage from @codemirror/language
   - Import toml from @codemirror/legacy-modes/mode/toml
   - Import linter, lintGutter from @codemirror/lint
   - Import oneDark from @codemirror/theme-one-dark
   - Import basicSetup from codemirror
   - Import parse from smol-toml for client-side validation
   - Component accepts props: content (string), onContentChange (callback), diagnostics (array)
   - Initialize CodeMirror with basicSetup, StreamLanguage.define(toml), oneDark, lintGutter()
   - Create a linter extension that:
     a. Calls smol-toml parse() for syntax validation (300ms debounce)
     b. Maps parse errors to CodeMirror Diagnostic[] with from/to positions
     c. Also maps server-side diagnostics (from props) to CodeMirror Diagnostics
   - Use createEditorControlledValue for reactive content updates
   - Export the component

2. Create dashboard/src/views/ConfigView.tsx:
   - Fetch config from GET /api/config on mount
   - Display ConfigEditor with loaded content
   - On content change:
     a. Debounce 800ms
     b. POST /api/config/validate with the content
     c. Pass validation errors to ConfigEditor as diagnostics
   - Save button:
     a. Enabled only when content is valid (no syntax or semantic errors)
     b. Shows spinner during save
     c. PUT /api/config with content and hash
     d. Handle 409 Conflict: show modal asking user to reload or overwrite
     e. On success: update hash, show success toast
   - Status bar showing: "Valid" (green), "N errors" (red), or "Validating..." (yellow)

3. Add API functions to dashboard/src/api.ts (or dashboard/src/lib/api.ts):
   - getConfig(): Promise<{content: string, path: string, hash: string}>
   - validateConfig(content: string): Promise<{valid: boolean, errors?: DiagnosticInfo[]}>
   - saveConfig(content: string, hash: string): Promise<{hash: string}>

4. Update dashboard/src/components/Sidebar.tsx:
   - Add Config nav item: { label: 'Config', icon: '\u2699', route: '#/config', match: (r) => r.startsWith('/config') }

5. Update dashboard/src/App.tsx:
   - Import ConfigView
   - Add Match for routeSegment() === 'config' rendering ConfigView
   - Add 'config' case to routeSegment() function

6. Rebuild: cd dashboard && npm run build

name: Skill install integration tests
description:
Create tests/integration/skill_install.rs with integration tests:

1. skill_install_project_local:
   - Create a TempDir with a minimal devrig.toml
   - Run `devrig skill install -f {dir}/devrig.toml` via Command
   - Assert dir/.claude/skills/devrig/SKILL.md exists
   - Read the file and assert it contains 'devrig' and 'query traces'

2. skill_install_global:
   - Create a TempDir for HOME
   - Create a TempDir with a minimal devrig.toml
   - Run `devrig skill install --global -f {dir}/devrig.toml` with HOME env override
   - Assert HOME/.claude/skills/devrig/SKILL.md exists

3. skill_install_idempotent:
   - Install twice to the same location
   - Assert second install succeeds
   - Assert content is identical between runs

4. skill_commands_are_valid:
   - Read the embedded SKILL.md content (or the file from skill/claude-code/SKILL.md)
   - Extract all `devrig <subcommand>` patterns via regex
   - For each unique subcommand, run `devrig {subcommand} --help` and assert exit code 0 or the stderr doesn't contain 'unrecognized'

5. Update tests/integration.rs to add:
   ```rust
   #[path = "integration/skill_install.rs"]
   mod skill_install;
   ```

name: Addon lifecycle integration tests
description:
Create tests/integration/addon_lifecycle.rs with integration tests:

1. addon_helm_install_and_cleanup:
   - Guard: skip if k3d or helm not available (k3d_available() && helm_available())
   - Create a devrig.toml with [cluster] and [cluster.addons.traefik] configured as a Helm addon:
     ```toml
     [cluster]
     registry = true
     
     [cluster.addons.traefik]
     type = "helm"
     chart = "traefik/traefik"
     repo = "https://traefik.github.io/charts"
     namespace = "traefik"
     port_forward = { 9000 = "svc/traefik:9000" }
     
     [cluster.addons.traefik.values]
     "ports.web.nodePort" = 32080
     ```
   - Use tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(300)) for the whole test
   - Start devrig (spawn child process)
   - Wait for cluster to be ready (poll kubectl get nodes)
   - Verify Traefik namespace exists (kubectl get ns traefik)
   - Verify Traefik pods are running (kubectl get pods -n traefik)
   - Verify port-forward works: wait_for_port(9000) with timeout
   - Send delete signal, verify port 9000 is released
   - Cleanup: k3d_cleanup_sync()

2. Add helm_available() helper to tests/common/mod.rs:
   - Run `helm version --short`, return true if exit code 0

3. Update tests/integration.rs to add:
   ```rust
   #[path = "integration/addon_lifecycle.rs"]
   mod addon_lifecycle;
   ```

name: Config editor E2E tests
description:
Create e2e/dashboard/config-editor.test.ts with Playwright tests:

1. config_editor_loads_current_config:
   - Navigate to /#/config
   - Wait for .cm-editor to be visible (timeout: 5000ms)
   - Verify cm-content contains '[project]' (the TOML section header)

2. shows_validation_error_for_invalid_config:
   - Navigate to /#/config
   - Wait for editor to load
   - Select all text (Ctrl+A) and type invalid TOML: '[project\nname = invalid'
   - Wait 1500ms for debounced validation
   - Assert .cm-lint-error markers are visible

3. save_button_disabled_when_invalid:
   - Navigate to /#/config
   - Type invalid content
   - Assert save button is disabled (has disabled attribute or disabled class)

4. config_nav_item_visible:
   - Navigate to /
   - Assert sidebar contains a 'Config' link
   - Click it
   - Assert URL hash is #/config

Note: CodeMirror renders content in <div class="cm-content"> not a <textarea>. Use keyboard.press('Control+a') + keyboard.type() instead of fill().
validation: cd /home/steve/fj/devrig/e2e && npx playwright test --list 2>/dev/null | grep -c config-editor

name: Create claude-code-skill guide
description:
Create docs/guides/claude-code-skill.md:

1. Overview section: what the devrig skill is and what it enables
2. Installation section:
   - Project-local: `devrig skill install` (preferred for team projects)
   - Global: `devrig skill install --global`
   - Verify: check .claude/skills/devrig/SKILL.md exists
3. What Claude can do section:
   - Investigate performance issues
   - Debug errors and failures
   - Check system health
   - Correlate traces, logs, and metrics
   - Inspect service configuration
4. Example prompts section:
   - "Why is the API slow?"
   - "What's erroring in the worker service?"
   - "Show me what happened at 10:30am"
   - "Is postgres healthy?"
   - "What services are running and are there any errors?"
5. How it works section:
   - Skill discovery mechanism (frontmatter loaded at startup)
   - Commands Claude uses (devrig query, devrig ps, devrig env)
   - Auto-approval via allowed-tools

name: Update configuration guide with addons
description:
Update docs/guides/configuration.md to add the [cluster.addons.*] section:

1. Add a 'Cluster Addons' section after the existing [cluster.deploy.*] section
2. Document the three addon types with examples:
   - Helm: full example with chart, repo, namespace, values, version, port_forward
   - Manifest: example with path and namespace
   - Kustomize: example with path
3. Document the port_forward field syntax
4. Document the values field and how TOML values map to helm --set
5. Document addon lifecycle:
   - Installed during cluster creation
   - Preserved across stop/start
   - Removed on delete
6. Add a Traefik example as the recommended ingress addon
7. Document the addon installation order (alphabetical by key name)
8. Mention the devrig doctor check for helm

name: Final build verification and formatting
description:
Run the complete build and quality checks:

1. cargo fmt -- apply formatting to all files
2. cargo clippy -- -D warnings -- fix any warnings
3. cargo build -- full build
4. cargo test -- run all unit tests (should be 211+ with new addon tests)
5. cd dashboard && npm run build -- rebuild frontend with config editor
6. Verify all new files exist:
   - skill/claude-code/SKILL.md
   - src/commands/skill.rs
   - src/cluster/addon.rs
   - src/dashboard/routes/config.rs
   - dashboard/src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx
   - dashboard/src/views/ConfigView.tsx
   - e2e/dashboard/config-editor.test.ts
   - tests/integration/skill_install.rs
   - tests/integration/addon_lifecycle.rs
   - docs/guides/claude-code-skill.md
7. Verify backwards compatibility: existing configs without addons still parse and validate
validation: cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo build && cargo test -- --quiet && cd /home/steve/fj/devrig/dashboard && npm run build
